title: Deaths from road injuries
subtitle: Annual number of deaths from road injuries. Road injuries include deaths of drivers, passengers, pedestrians and cyclists.
caption:
Deaths from road injuries

Annual number of deaths from road injuries. Road injuries include deaths of drivers, passengers, pedestrians and cyclists.

This choropleth map displays the annual number of deaths from road injuries across countries in 2023, using a continuous color scale ranging from light beige for zero deaths to deep maroon for 100,000 deaths. The visualization reveals a distinct geographic distribution where the highest mortality figures concentrate in populous nations across Asia and the Americas. China and India appear in the darkest maroon band, indicating figures at or exceeding the 100,000 threshold, representing the maximum values visible on the map. The United States and Brazil occupy the second-highest tier, shaded in a medium-dark red that corresponds to the 30,000 to 100,000 range. A broad cluster of countries including Russia, Mexico, Indonesia, and several nations in Eastern Europe and Southern Africa fall within the 10,000 to 30,000 band, shown in orange. Lower values between 3,000 and 10,000 cover much of Western Europe, Canada, Australia, and parts of South America, depicted in lighter orange shades. The lightest beige tones, representing fewer than 1,000 deaths, appear across smaller nations and sparsely populated regions such as Greenland and Iceland. Several areas, primarily in central Africa and parts of the Middle East, display a hatched pattern denoting no available data. The data shows that the absolute number of fatalities scales with population size and geographic extent, with the most intense colors appearing in the world's most populous regions while vast areas of the globe remain in the lower bands.
